_meta.notes: Case study pages combine project-style structure with a longer-form narrative. Layout: hero image, title H1, lead intro paragraph, 'Project Info' sidebar (Location, Owner/Client, Project Type, Completion Date, sometimes Award), then multiple H3-led narrative sections each with body prose and image gallery, and (rarely) team-member spotlights. Two URL shapes exist: /case-study/{slug}/ (slash) and /case-study-{slug}/ (flat-dash) — same template, separate entries per the zero-301s constraint.

prompt: Extract a warehausae.com case study page. Capture title, lead intro, the Project Info sidebar facts, every named narrative section (heading + body + images), and any award or team callout. Preserve image URLs and captions.
systemPrompt: Extracting one case study page on warehausae.com. Use null or empty arrays for missing fields. Do not invent narrative text.

hero_image_url.description: URL of the hero image at the top of the page.

intro_prose.description: Paragraph(s) immediately following the title, joined with double newlines.

owner_client.description: Owner/Client text (may include multiple lines like 'Engineer of Record / for New Freedom Borough').

award.description: Award text if displayed in the sidebar (e.g. 'Preservation Pennsylvania Award Winner').

heading.description: H3 heading of this narrative section.

body.description: Body prose of the section, joined with double newlines.

sections.description: Named narrative sections under H3 headings.

title_line.description: Role line shown below the name (e.g. 'Sewage Enforcement Officer / SEO# 04183').

team_spotlights.description: Team-member callouts that appear in some case studies (e.g. the SEO Stephen Stone block on /case-study/municipal-engineering/).

related_project_url.description: If the case study links out to a /project/{slug}/ page, that URL.
